blocks:
Surah Al-Asr

Meccan, and it consists of three verses by consensus, with two verses having variation. *وَالْعَصْرِ ** (The Time) is not counted by the Meccan and the last Medinan schools. *بِالْحَقِ ** (with the truth) is counted by the Meccan and the last Medinan schools. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالْعَصْرِ (1) By the Time, (٢) إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ ** (2) man is indeed in loss, It was said that He swore by the ʿAsr prayer (صلاة العصر) or by the era of Prophethood (عصر النبوّة), that man is indeed in loss (خسران) regarding their endeavors and the expenditure of their lifespans in their pursuits. (٣) إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ ** (3) except those who have faith and do righteous deeds, For they have purchased the Hereafter with the world, thus achieving eternal life (الحياة الأبديّة) and everlasting bliss (السعادة السرمديّة). وَتَواصَوْا بِالْحَقِ ** and enjoin one another to [adhere to] the truth, which is the established truth (الثابت) whose denial is not permissible, whether in belief (اعتقاد) or action (عمل). وَتَواصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ ** and enjoin one another to patience. [Patience] from sins (المعاصي), and upon acts of obedience (الطّاعات) and calamities (المصائب). And this is an instance of joining the specific (الخاص) to the general (العام).

In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: الْعَصْرِ ** (The Time) means the era of the emergence of the Qā’im (peace be upon him). إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ ** (man is indeed in loss) means our enemies. إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (except those who have faith) means those who have faith in Our signs (بآياتنا). وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ ** (and do righteous deeds) means by comforting the brethren (بمواساة الاخوان). وَتَواصَوْا بِالْحَقِ ** (and enjoin one another to [adhere to] the truth) means the Imamate (الإمامة). وَتَواصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ ** (and enjoin one another to patience) means the ʿItrah (العترة) [the Prophet's progeny].

And al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), said: He (Allah) excluded the people of His chosen ones (أهل صفوته) from His creation when He said: إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ** (man is indeed in loss, except those who have faith). He says: They believed in the Wilāyah (ولاية) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), وَتَواصَوْا بِالْحَقِ (and enjoin one another to [adhere to] the truth) their descendants and those they left behind, they enjoined them to the Wilāyah (الولاية) and were patient upon it.

In al-Majmaʿ, narrated from ʿAli (peace be upon him), and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They both recited: وَالْعَصْرِ إِنَّ الْإِنْسانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ (By the Time, man is indeed in loss) ilā ākhiri al-dahr (إلى آخر الدّهر) (until the end of time).

In Thawāb al-Aʿmāl and al-Majmaʿ, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites وَالْعَصْرِ (By the Time) in his supererogatory prayers (نوافله), Allah will raise him on the Day of Resurrection with his face shining (مشرقاً وجهه), his teeth smiling (ضاحكاً سنّه), and his eye delighted (قريراً عينه), until He admits him into Paradise.
